_meta.description: Scene Nodes knowledge base for the cinema4d-mcp plugin. Categorizes the node template vocabulary observed across 9 example scenes (Squiggle Spline, Time Offset, Explode Spline Segments, Scaffolds, Whirlpool, City Generator, Fractal Trees, Balloon Inflate, Edge to Spline + Ivy Generator). The 'live_label_index' is filled by Phase A1 enumeration against the running C4D's user prefs repo.
_meta.$type_contract: ApplyDescription's $type field accepts CASE-SENSITIVE English UI labels (e.g. 'Sphere', 'Inset', 'Loop Carried Value'). Canonical asset IDs (e.g. 'net.maxon.neutron.node.primitive.sphere') do NOT work directly. Bare base-names extracted from existing graphs (e.g. 'extrude', 'inset') are LOCAL graph instance names, NOT template labels — they fail too.

live_label_index._format: { canonical_asset_id: { label: 'UI English label', namespace: 'neutron|redshift|node|user', port_summary: {...} } }
live_label_index._status: pending — populated when scripts/build_atlas.py is run against a live C4D

framework_nodes._description: Auto-generated wrapper nodes that appear in EVERY functional capsule — boilerplate, never user-added.
framework_nodes.context_externaltimeinput: Provides time context to the graph (frame number, time)
framework_nodes.context_notime: Provides a time-less evaluation context for non-animated paths
framework_nodes.parambuilder: Wraps user-facing capsule parameters; reads from the host BaseObject
framework_nodes.modelingoperator: Wraps a graph that behaves as a classic-object modeling operator (deformer/generator)
framework_nodes.generategeometry: Output sink for generators — emits the resulting BaseObject
framework_nodes.defaultselections: Supplies fallback selection sets when none is named
framework_nodes.net.maxon.neutron.scene.root: Root container of the scene-nodes branch in a doc-level graph

visual_organization._description: Pure layout/documentation primitives. Pass data through unchanged; safe to ignore for semantic analysis.
visual_organization.scaffold: A clickable frame that visually groups regions of the editor. Functional pass-through.
visual_organization.annotation: Editor-only sticky note. No data flow.
visual_organization.group: Collapsible bubble that hides/shows contained nodes. Pass-through.
visual_organization.reroute: Visual cable redirect, like a hub — single in, single out, identity.
visual_organization.floatingio: Graph boundary I/O attachment point — exposes a port to the parent capsule's parameter UI.
visual_organization.text: Text label on the canvas. Editor-only.
visual_organization.type: Type declaration scaffold — ports adopt the declared type.

loop_over_indices.description: Generic indexed iteration. Run a body N times, optionally carry state. The minimum viable Scene Nodes loop.
loop_over_indices.skeleton_text: loopcarriedvalue + start + end + range (driven by getcount) + readvalueatindex (per-iter read) + writevalueatindex (per-iter write)
loop_over_indices.carried_state_recipe: One Loop Carried Value per state variable. Initial value via 'init' port; update via 'next' port; read prior via 'current' port.

loop_over_polygons.description: Iterate every polygon of an input mesh, run a body, accumulate output. Concrete loop over a known dimension.
loop_over_polygons.skeleton_text: getpolygonselectiondata → getcount → range → readvalueatindex (poly index) → polygonarrayget (per-poly attrs) → [body] → writevalueatindex / append (output)

loop_over_points.description: Iterate every point of an input mesh.

loop_over_spline_segments.description: Iterate spline line segments. Common for spline modifiers.
loop_over_spline_segments.skeleton_text: spline → getcount → range → lineget → [body] → lineset / assembler

reaction_diffusion_on_geometry.description: Two-buffer chemical-concentration update per frame. Memory nodes hold U and V across frames. The classic Gray-Scott pattern.
reaction_diffusion_on_geometry.skeleton_text: 2× memory (U, V buffers) → get_property (read prior) → arithmetic (diffusion + reaction terms) → blend (smoothing) → set_property (write back) → memory.next (carry to next frame)

surface_clinging_growth.description: Branches that grow while staying close to a host mesh surface. Each step: snap to surface, raycast, choose direction, advance.
surface_clinging_growth.skeleton_text: loopcarriedvalue × N (carry pos+dir+age+depth) → closestpointonsurface (snap to host) → ray (collision check) → hash + arithmetic (random direction) → compare/if (continue/fork/terminate) → matrixfromaxis + composematrix (local frame) → append → next iteration

stochastic_branching_decision.description: Hash-driven random decisions per loop step. Used for fractal/L-system patterns.
stochastic_branching_decision.skeleton_text: hash (deterministic random) → compare (vs threshold) → if/switch (branch) → [different sub-paths]

fractal_recursion_via_stacking.description: Achieve recursion by stacking N instances of the same deformer on the same input. Each instance applies one level.
fractal_recursion_via_stacking.skeleton_text: single capsule template, dragged N times into the same parent → stacked deformers compose like function composition
fractal_recursion_via_stacking.note: Scene Nodes does NOT support true recursion within a single graph. Stacking is the workaround.

partition_split.description: Stochastically split each polygon into two via a random line cut. Used for procedural city / surface fracturing.
partition_split.skeleton_text: for each poly: hash → randomized cut line → distancetoline classify points → clamp → subdivide where line crosses → assemble two new polys

spline_break_by_threshold.description: Insert control points on a spline wherever a per-point metric (angle change, segment length) exceeds threshold, then split.
spline_break_by_threshold.skeleton_text: loop over spline points → measure angle/length → compare vs threshold → addcontrolpointalongspline (add a point) → splitspline (cut)

spline_resample_with_displacement.description: Resample a spline at constant arc-length, then displace each sample by time-varying noise.
spline_resample_with_displacement.skeleton_text: spline → splinedistnode (arc-length parameterize) → step (uniform sampling) → loop sample points → noise(pos, time) → transformmatrix → emit displaced point

object_instancing_with_variation.description: Generate N instances of a child object, each with parametric variation driven by a hash + index.
object_instancing_with_variation.skeleton_text: objectimport (read child object) → range → readvalueatindex → hash(index) → noise + arithmetic (per-instance values) → sqrtrans/vectrans/sqrpart/combine (transform) → cloner emits

per_vertex_property_storage_and_recall.description: Tag each vertex with a named scalar/vector value, then read it back later in the graph or in a downstream capsule.
per_vertex_property_storage_and_recall.skeleton_text: set_property name='density' value=<expr> → ... downstream ... → get_property name='density' → use as input to another op

mesh_element_query_by_selection.description: Take a user-typed selection name → resolve to actual selection → bulk-extract attributes (position, normal, color, weight) from the selected polys/points.
mesh_element_query_by_selection.skeleton_text: floatingio (sel name from UI) → selectionstringparser → getpolygonselectiondata → 'frompolyids' family bulk extracts → assembler

selection_evolution_chain.description: Chain of selection ops (random → grow → invert → store named) used between modeling steps.
selection_evolution_chain.skeleton_text: randomselection N% → growselection by k → invertselection → setselection name='out'

modular_polygon_selection.description: Select polygons by modular arithmetic on their index. The Modulo capsule pattern: every Nth polygon, with optional offset and AND/OR combination of multiple mod conditions.
modular_polygon_selection.skeleton_text: polygonarrayget → containeriteration × 2 (nested) → for each poly: readvalueatindex (poly idx) + length (count) → modulo × N (one per condition) → compare × N → switch tree (combine logic) → buildselection → setselection (named output)
observed_in: Modulo capsule (net.maxon.neutron.asset.geo.modulo, 84 nodes / 56 unique bases / 6 levels)

dual_mesh_topology_transform.description: Convert a mesh into its graph-theoretic dual: every poly→vert, every vert→poly, every edge→edge. The Dual Mesh Modifier capsule pattern.
dual_mesh_topology_transform.skeleton_text: input mesh → polygoninfo (centroids, per-poly) → geoselectionrange × N → extrude+inset+melt to spawn vertex per poly center → pointinfo (neighbors, per-vert) → indexarrayfromstring × N to build per-vert poly-ring selections → connect ring-of-poly-centers → polygonbevel + triangulate → delete original geometry → output dual mesh
observed_in: Dual Mesh Modifier capsule (138 nodes / 46 unique bases / 7 levels)

dynamic_selection_naming.description: Build per-element selection names at runtime using string.concat — e.g. 'dual_v_42' per vertex. Lets a graph generate uniquely-named selections that downstream operators can reference by name even when count is dynamic.
dynamic_selection_naming.skeleton_text: index/idx → string.concat with prefix → indexarrayfromstring → selectionstringtoselection → setselection
observed_in: Dual Mesh Modifier (5× string.concat for runtime-named selections)

named_selection_storage_triad.description: The canonical 3-node sub-pattern that appears every time you store a named selection inside a capsule — selectionstringtoselection + selectionoperator + variadictolist.
named_selection_storage_triad.skeleton_text: variadictolist (collect inputs) → selectionoperator (apply op) → selectionstringtoselection (resolve named ref) → setselection (commit)
named_selection_storage_triad.rule_of_thumb: If your pattern produces a selection, expect 1-6× this triad in the inner graph.

port_type_taxonomy._description: Common port data types and known conversion paths. When a port-type mismatch occurs, use the suggested converter node.

convert_from.Vector: use 'vectortofloat' (extracts magnitude or component) OR access X/Y/Z component port directly

convert_from.Float64: use 'composevector3' (x, y, z all to same scalar) OR set ONE component leave others 0

Geometry.description: Polygon mesh data — points, polys, edges, attributes

Spline.description: Curve data — control points, segments, knots, tangents

Container.description: Heterogeneous typed bundle — multiple named sub-values

convert_from.any: no automatic conversion — see selectionstringparser for parsing

instance_hash_suffix.meaning: When a node has parameter values set on it, it gets a unique instance ID. Same template + different params = same basename + different hash.

bare_name.meaning: A node WITHOUT @hash suffix is a sub-node living inside a compound operator (parambuilder, modelingoperator, etc.) or a scaffold/visual primitive. NOT addable directly via add_node — only appears nested inside another node.

modeling_op_internals.description: Modeling operators (Inset, Extrude, etc.) ALWAYS contain these inner nodes after instantiation:

capsule_build_template._description: Universal skeleton present in EVERY functional capsule. The 6 framework nodes wrap any user logic.
capsule_build_template.user_logic_zone: Sits between read of input geometry and emission via generategeometry. The user's algorithm goes here.
capsule_build_template.exposed_params: Each user-facing parameter = one floatingio node attached to a port on the modelingoperator.

_design_principle_capsule_first.rule: Scene Nodes graphs exist to be CONSUMED by the object tree as Generators/Deformers (capsules), with user-data parameters exposed in the Attribute Manager. Bare graphs are intermediate artifacts; capsules with exposed UD are the artist deliverable.
_design_principle_capsule_first.why: Artists work in the object tree. They drag generators/deformers, parameterize them via the Attribute Manager, never re-open the underlying graph. Scene Nodes' value comes from being the ENGINE behind a capsule — not from being the artist-facing UI itself.
implications_for_pattern_library: Every functional pattern should default to materializing inside an SN Generator (180420500/600/700) or SN Deformer (180420400) wrapper.
Patterns must auto-emit `floatingio` ports for each tunable parameter (count, threshold, seed, etc.) so they appear in the host capsule's user-data section of the Attribute Manager.
When wrapping: the pattern's bound parameters connect from `floatingio` source ports to the relevant inner-node input ports.
Recommended workflow: scene_nodes_apply_pattern(pattern, graph_target=<existing_capsule_name>) — adds the pattern INSIDE that capsule's embedded graph.

create_a_new_capsule_pattern.description: Creates a fresh SN Deformer or SN Generator and a graph populated by the named pattern, with UD already exposed.
skeleton: 1. doc.InsertObject(c4d.BaseObject(180420400 or 180420700))  # SN Deformer or Generator
4. Add `floatingio` node per exposed param, $name'd to the AttributeManager-visible field name
5. Connect `floatingio` outputs to the pattern's inner port inputs
6. The capsule auto-renders its UD in the Attribute Manager via the parambuilder framework node
create_a_new_capsule_pattern.advantages: Single-step from intent to artist-ready capsule. Artist drags it in, sets parameters via Attribute Manager, never touches the graph.

$type_label_dictionary._description: Verified mapping between dissection bare names, canonical asset IDs, and ApplyDescription $type labels. KEY RULE confirmed via user-supplied canonical IDs (2026-04-29): the bare-name extracted from a dissection (e.g. 'memory' from 'memory@HASH') is ALWAYS the last segment of the canonical asset ID (e.g. 'net.maxon.node.memory'). So `dissection_basename = canonical_id.rsplit('.', 1)[-1]`.
$type_label_dictionary.label_resolution_strategy: 1. Look up bare_name in _verified_working below. 2. If miss: take the canonical asset_id from node_template_index.json (matched by last segment) and use the heuristic-derived label_guess. 3. ApplyDescription may need the LABEL form, not the canonical id — when that fails, use scene_nodes_atlas_lookup to fuzzy-find candidates.

_user_supplied_canonical_ids._note: User-confirmed canonical asset IDs from C4D's asset browser (2026-04-29). These match dissection bare names by rsplit('.', 1)[-1].

Build Array.note: the artist-facing label is 'Build Array' even though the canonical last-segment is 'buildfromvalue'

Append.label_status: unresolved — none of {Append, Append Array, Add To Array, append2} work as $type

Concat.label_status: AMBIGUOUS — 'Concatenate' returns disambig list of these two; no working $type form found yet

Modulo (Geo).label_status: AMBIGUOUS — domain-suffix forms (Geometry Modulo, Geo Modulo) all fail

_critical_keyword_vs_template_distinctio.rule: Asset browser entries come in MULTIPLE TYPES. ApplyDescription's $type ONLY accepts NodeTemplate-typed entries — NOT Keyword entries.
_critical_keyword_vs_template_distinctio.evidence: User-supplied: 'Set Selection' has Asset ID 'keyword@0656a456b8bf467981f8d0b439142e2c' and Type ID 'net.maxon.assettype.keyword' — it's a search/category KEYWORD, not a template. The actual template that does set-selection is labeled 'Store Selection' (verified working).
_critical_keyword_vs_template_distinctio.implication: When labels look reasonable but fail with 'not associated with any IDs', the entry might be a keyword. Look for an adjacent template with a slightly different label that does the same conceptual operation.

thin_capsules_observed._description: Capsules that contain 0-3 user-visible nodes — they're just UI wrappers around hidden built-in operations.

Inset.function: wraps the modeling op + inset operator

Extrude.function: wraps the modeling op + extrude operator

antipatterns_and_traps.name: passing_canonical_asset_id_to_$type
antipatterns_and_traps.reason: $type expects the case-sensitive English UI label, not the asset registry ID.

antipatterns_and_traps.name: assuming_dissection_ids_are_addable
antipatterns_and_traps.wrong: Take 'extrude@HASH' from dissect_capsule output → use as $type
antipatterns_and_traps.right: Strip hash → 'extrude' is the base name → use the LABEL form 'Extrude' instead
antipatterns_and_traps.reason: Dissection returns local graph instance names. Only the canonicalized English label works in $type.

antipatterns_and_traps.name: trying_to_add_inner_subnodes_directly
antipatterns_and_traps.right: Add the OUTER operator (e.g. 'Inset') and the inner nodes appear automatically.
antipatterns_and_traps.reason: parambuilder/modelingoperator/etc. are sub-nodes of compound operators, not addable templates.

antipatterns_and_traps.name: running_GraphDescription_from_worker_thread
antipatterns_and_traps.wrong: Calling GraphDescription.GetGraph(host) from a non-main thread
antipatterns_and_traps.reason: GraphDescription requires main thread; worker calls error with 'must be run from the main thread'

antipatterns_and_traps.name: expecting_recursion_in_a_single_graph
antipatterns_and_traps.wrong: Trying to make a graph reference itself
antipatterns_and_traps.right: Stack N copies of a deformer (Fractal Trees pattern), or use loopcarriedvalue with iteration_count to simulate depth-bounded recursion
antipatterns_and_traps.reason: Scene Nodes is not a recursive language.
